description_long: This is a medium-sized, roughly rectangular rock with a rugged, uneven surface and prominent striations of gray, white, and black minerals running across its length. The rock displays a weathered appearance, with jagged edges and a coarse texture, suggesting it has been naturally shaped or minimally processed. On one of its flatter faces, there is a visible black marking or label, possibly written with a marker, indicating human interaction or cataloging. The coloration is mottled, with streaks of lighter and darker minerals interspersed, giving it a distinctive, layered look. The rock's mass and density are apparent from its solid, blocky form, and it appears heavy and difficult to move without effort. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban park, construction site, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, rectangular rock with gray, white, and black mineral striations and a visible black marking on one face. Its coarse, weathered surface and solid form make it a natural obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the top and one long side of the rock, highlighting the black marking and the layered mineral striations.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a jagged, angular end and the continuation of the mineral bands, with less visible marking.
description_view_2: The side profile displays a mostly rough, dark surface with less pronounced striations and no visible markings.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rock's thickness and the prominent mineral layers, with the marking partially visible along the top edge.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
